content.problemStatement:
You are given positive integers NNN and MMM (M≥2)(M \geq 2)(M≥2).

You are also given an array AAA of size NNN, such that 2≤Ai≤M2 \leq A_i \leq M2≤Ai​≤M.

In one operation on the array, you can:


Select an integer XXX (2≤X≤M)(2 \leq X \leq M)(2≤X≤M) and change AiA_iAi​ to gcd⁡(Ai,X)\gcd(A_i,X)gcd(Ai​,X) for **all** 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N.

Note that gcd⁡\gcdgcd denotes the greatest common divisor.
Your task is to make all the elements of AAA **equal**.

Find the **minimum** number of operations required to do so and also print the selected integer XXX for each operation.

If there are multiple ways to do so, you may print any.

It can be proven that it is always possible to make all the elements of AAA equal in finite number of operations.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and MMM — the length of the array AAA and maximum value of XXX allowed.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​,  representing the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, in the first line, print an integer ZZZ, denoting the number of operations required.


In case Z>0Z\gt 0Z>0, print another line with ZZZ space-separated integers where the ithi^{th}ith integer represents the selected integer XXX selected in the ithi^{th}ith operation in **order**.


If there are multiple ways to do so, you may print any.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.
Note that sum of MMM over all test case isn't bounded.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Since all elements of the array are already equal, we require 000 operations.
Test case 222: We require only one operation:

Select X=4X = 4X=4: Thus, the array becomes [gcd⁡(4,4),gcd⁡(4,8),gcd⁡(4,12),gcd⁡(4,16),gcd⁡(4,20)]=[4,4,4,4,4][\gcd(4, 4), \gcd(4, 8), \gcd(4, 12), \gcd(4, 16), \gcd(4, 20)] = [4, 4, 4, 4, 4][gcd(4,4),gcd(4,8),gcd(4,12),gcd(4,16),gcd(4,20)]=[4,4,4,4,4], where all elements are equal.

Note that this is the minimum number of operations required.
